subtopics: Equation of tangent & normal to a curve

questions.question: The slope of the tangent to the curve y = f(x) at point (x1, y1) is given by:
The slope of the normal to the curve y = f(x) at point (x1, y1) is:
The equation of the tangent at (x1, y1) is y - y1 = m(x - x1), where m is ______.
The diagram show a curve and a line touching it at a single point. This line is the ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve with a straight line making contact at exactly one point P(x,y).

questions.question: The diagram show a line perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact. This line is the ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve, a tangent line, and a second line through the same point P making a 90-degree angle with the tangent.

questions.question: If the tangent is parallel to the x-axis, then dy/dx = ______.
If the tangent is parallel to the y-axis, then dy/dx = ______.
The angle between two curves at their point of intersection is the angle between their ______ at that point.
If two curves intersect orthogonally, the product of their slopes m1, m2 at the point of intersection is ______.
The diagram show two curves intersecting. The angle ψ is given by tan ψ = ______.
questions.diagram_description: Two intersecting curves with their tangent lines drawn, showing an angle ψ between the tangents.

questions.question: Find the slope of the tangent to y = x² at x = 2.
The equation of the normal to y = x³ at (1, 1) is:
The length of the tangent at point (x1, y1) on curve y=f(x) is ______.
The diagram show the sub-tangent of a curve. Its length is given by ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve, a tangent, and its projection on the x-axis representing the sub-tangent.

questions.question: The length of the normal at point (x1, y1) is ______.
The diagram show the sub-normal of a curve. Its length is given by ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve, a normal line, and its projection on the x-axis representing the sub-normal.

questions.question: A point on the curve where the tangent is horizontal is a ______ point.
The diagram show a curve with a vertical tangent. The derivative dy/dx at this point is ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve that becomes infinitely steep at a specific point x.

questions.question: If the slope of the tangent at (x, y) is y/x, the curve is a ______.
The slope of the normal to xy = c² at (c, c) is ______.
If two curves y = f(x) and y = g(x) touch each other at (x1, y1), then f'(x1) ______ g'(x1).
The distance of the tangent at origin to the curve y² = 4ax is ______.
The diagram show an angle θ that the tangent at (x, y) makes with positive x-axis. Then m = ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve and its tangent line making an angle θ with the horizontal axis.

questions.question: Find the slope of the normal to y² = 8x at (2, 4).
The equation of the tangent to x²/a² + y²/b² = 1 at (x1, y1) is ______.
The angle between curves y² = 4ax and x² = 4ay at (4a, 4a) is ______.
The diagram show a curve and two parallel tangents. At these points, the derivative dy/dx must be ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve with two separate tangent lines that are parallel to each other.

questions.question: If the sub-tangent is constant, the curve is a ______.
If the sub-normal is constant, the curve is a ______.
The diagram show a curve and its tangent. The tangent from a point (h, k) outside satisfies ______.
questions.diagram_description: A point outside a parabola with two tangents drawn to the curve.

questions.question: Find the slope of tangent to y = sin x at x = 0.
The normal to a circle at any point always passes through its ______.
Slope of the tangent to a curve y = f(x) at origin if it passes through (0,0) is often simply the coefficient of ______ in the expansion.
The diagram show a curve where the tangent is vertical. This occurs when dx/dy = ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve that is 'falling' vertically at one point.

questions.question: The point on y = x² where tangent is parallel to y = 4x - 5 is ______.
The angle between y = x² and y² = x at (1, 1) is ______.
The diagram show two orthogonal curves. Their tangents are ______ to each other.
questions.diagram_description: Two curves crossing with a small square symbol at the intersection, showing their tangents meet at 90 degrees.

questions.question: The equation of tangent to xy = 6 at (2, 3) is ______.
A curve y = f(x) has a normal parallel to x-axis at (x1, y1). Then f'(x1) is ______.
The diagram show a tangent line. The length of the intercept it makes on x-axis is given by x - ______.
questions.diagram_description: A tangent line at P(x,y) crossing the x-axis at a point T.

questions.question: Slope of the tangent to x² + y² = 25 at (3, 4) is ______.
Find the equation of tangent to y = e^x at (0, 1).
The diagram show a curve and its normal at P. The normal makes an angle φ with x-axis. Then tan φ = ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve, a normal line, and an angle φ with the horizontal.

questions.question: Angle between y = log x and x-axis at its intersection point (1, 0) is ______.
Two curves intersect at an angle psi. If psi = 0, the curves ______ each other.
The diagram show a curve segment. The length of normal is ______ in term of y and y'.
questions.diagram_description: Illustration of the length segment between the point on curve and the x-axis along the normal line.

questions.question: Slope of normal to y = cos x at x = π/2 is ______.
The intercept made by normal on y-axis is y + ______.
The diagram show a curve with sub-tangent equal to abcissa (x). The curve is an ______.
questions.diagram_description: A curve where the sub-tangent OT equals the x-coordinate of P.

questions.question: The slope of tangent to x = a cos³t, y = a sin³t at t = π/4 is ______.
